name: Friendship Cake
recipeInstructions:
Day 1: Pour starter into a glass gallon jar. Add a can of sliced peaches with the juice; cut each slice into 4 pieces. Add 2 1/2 cups sugar. Stir. Cover jar loosely and keep at room temperature. Stir every day for 10 days. Do not cover airtight.
Day 10: Add a can of pineapple tidbits with juice and 2 1/2 cups sugar. Stir. Continue as before, stirring every day for 10 more days. Your starter will change color and the fruit will foam when stirred.
Day 20: Add 3 jars of maraschino cherries (drained), each cut in half. Add 2 1/2 cups sugar. Stir. Continue stirring daily for 10 days. The cherries will restore the pinkish color to the juice.
Day 31: Preheat oven to 300°F. Grease and flour pans. Drain fruit into a large bowl. Pour juice into three pint jars. In a large bowl, mix one box cake mix, one box instant vanilla pudding mix, four eggs, 2/3 cup oil, and half the drained fruit. Mix until well combined. Stir in 1 cup each of raisins, walnuts, pecans, and coconut. Pour into prepared pans. Bake until a straw inserted in the center comes out clean. Makes 2 tube pans or 12 one-pound loaves or 4 loaves, 9 ½ x 5 ½ x 3 inches. Bake one tube pan at a time for approximately 60-90 minutes. Large loaves require 60-90 minutes to bake until clean.
Comments: Must use fruit in juices, not syrup. The three pint jars of juice are the starters; one is yours and two are for your friends. Never refrigerate the starter!
